ocr.text: In the competitive landscape of the North American foodservice industry, Danone has strategically focused on optimizing its
performance metrics to enhance its market positioning. The foodservice channel has increasingly become a pivotal area for
growth, particularly as consumer preferences shift towards healthier and more sustainable dining options. The accompanying
performance data table provides a comprehensive overview of key metrics that illustrate Danone's operational efficiency and
profitability within this sector. Notably, the metrics of Net Income Margin and Debt/Equity are critical in assessing the

company's financial health and leverage as it navigates the intricacies of the foodservice market.

The Net Income Margin, which
reflects the percentage of revenue that translates into profit, is indicative of Danone's ability to manage costs effectively while
maximizing sales. A robust Net Income Margin, aligned with industry averages, suggests that Danone is not only generating
revenue but also converting a significant portion of that revenue into profit, a crucial factor for sustaining long-term growth.
Channel  Q12024  SalesGrowth  ProfitabilityMetrics  LiquidityRatios  MarketShare  CustomerSatisfaction
Retail  15%  20%  GrossMargin  CurrentRatio  DebtRatio  NetPromoterScore
Foodservice  10%  18%  EBITDAMargin  QuickRatio  EquityRatio  ServiceQuality
E-commerce  25%  22%  OperatingMargin  CashRatio  TotalDebt  CustomerRetention
Wholesale  12%  19%  NetIncomeMargin  Debt/Equity  CurrentLiabilities  BrandLoyalty  International  8%  15%
TotalSales  $300M  $140M  20%  2.0  25%  80%  3.0  15%  OverallPerformance
Conversely, the Debt/Equity ratio offers insights into the company's capital structure and financial stability, revealing how much
of the company's financing comes from debt versus equity. A balanced approach to leveraging debt can enable Danone to

reinvest in its foodservice operations, fostering innovation and enhancing brand loyalty among its consumers.

As the
foodservice landscape evolves, characterized by increasing competition and a growing emphasis on health-conscious offerings,
Danone's performance in this channel is more critical than ever. The data presented in the table also underscores the importance
of brand loyalty, with metrics indicating how well Danone's products resonate with consumers in a dining context. By
cultivating strong brand loyalty, Danone can not only drive repeat business but also enhance its market share in a sector that is

increasingly driven by consumer preferences and trends.

Moreover, the international dimension of Danone's foodservice
performance reflects its commitment to expanding its footprint beyond North American borders, leveraging its established
brand reputation to penetrate emerging markets. In summary, the metrics outlined in the table serve as a vital tool for analyzing
Danone's foodservice channel performance, providing stakeholders with insights into the company's operational efficiencies,
financial health, and strategic direction. As the industry continues to adapt, these insights will be instrumental in guiding
Danone's future initiatives and investments within the foodservice sector.
